title: Expand payment processor test coverage with failure and edge cases
author: eval-bot

context_units.body_old:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock
from app.payments.processor import PaymentProcessor


def test_charge_success():
    gateway = MagicMock()
    gateway.charge.return_value = {"status": "ok", "transaction_id": "txn_123"}
    proc = PaymentProcessor(gateway)
    result = proc.charge(amount=50_00, currency="USD", token="tok_abc")
    assert result["status"] == "ok"
    assert result["transaction_id"] == "txn_123"
context_units.body_new:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock
from app.payments.processor import PaymentError, PaymentProcessor


def test_charge_success():
    gateway = MagicMock()
    gateway.charge.return_value = {"status": "ok", "transaction_id": "txn_123"}
    proc = PaymentProcessor(gateway)
    result = proc.charge(amount=50_00, currency="USD", token="tok_abc")
    assert result["status"] == "ok"
    assert result["transaction_id"] == "txn_123"


def test_charge_gateway_failure_raises():
    gateway = MagicMock()
    gateway.charge.side_effect = RuntimeError("network timeout")
    proc = PaymentProcessor(gateway)
    with pytest.raises(PaymentError, match="network timeout"):
        proc.charge(amount=10_00, currency="USD", token="tok_xyz")


def test_charge_zero_amount_rejected():
    gateway = MagicMock()
    proc = PaymentProcessor(gateway)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be positive"):
        proc.charge(amount=0, currency="USD", token="tok_xyz")
    gateway.charge.assert_not_called()


def test_charge_negative_amount_rejected():
    gateway = MagicMock()
    proc = PaymentProcessor(gateway)
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be positive"):
        proc.charge(amount=-1, currency="USD", token="tok_xyz")
    gateway.charge.assert_not_called()
